content:
Goal
* I'm not sure whether the term "standard English keyboard layout" is appropriate because there isn't really a global standard. Besides, the keyboard consists of more than 26 keys. It may be clearer to call it the "QWERTY keyboard letter layout" instead.

* The keyboard illustration is slightly too wide (e.g. in my usual browser window). Please reduce every key's width by 2 space characters.

* Please change "their sides intersect" to "they share a side".

Cases
* All cases that expect a single letter as the output currently appear as validators only. Some of them should be rearranged as tests.

* Please reorder the cases (except the first pair of example cases) by increasing output length, grouping them in pairs with the same or similar output lengths. Of course, feel free to share if you have other considerations behind the current ordering.

Thanks.

Hi! Very good idea! I like it! :)

As it stands, it doesn't seem very suitable for the “shortest” mode, though. (Players could simply hardcode the 26 solutions)
To make this mode truly interesting, we could, for example, use a list of defective keys (that can't be used) as input instead of a standard full keyboard (just a thought).

EDIT: I hadn't seen your solution, it's actually pretty elegant.
I take back what I said; if we hard-coded it, the solution would be much longer :D

I allow the hardcoded method as a "cop-out" in case players can't figure out the dynamic programming solution :D

Didn't explicitly check the length of the hardcoded solution, but glad to hear that it's longer than mine. Thanks @N3I!
I've made the copy changes, will work on the test case ordering soon. Thanks @5DN1L!
